pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: High-level introduction to Zod: what it is, its monorepo structure, key design principles (zero dependencies, immutable API, TypeScript-first), and how to navigate the wiki. Links to child pages for getting started and the v4 release.

pages.title: Getting Startedd
pages.purpose: Installation, basic usage (defining schemas, parsing data, handling errors, type inference with z.infer), and the difference between Zod and Zod Mini. Covers the pnpm monorepo setup and development workflow.

pages.title: Zod v4 Migration and Changelog
pages.purpose: Documents what changed in Zod v4 vs v3, the versioning strategy (zod/v3 and zod/v4 subpaths), breaking changes, and the compatibility layer (compat.ts). Covers the incremental rollout approach.

pages.title: Core Architecture
pages.purpose: Overview of the internal architecture: the $ZodType base class, the $constructor pattern, the _zod internals property, and how the parsing pipeline flows. Links to child pages for schemas, checks, parsing, and utilities.

pages.title: Schema Types
pages.purpose: Detailed reference for all built-in schema types: primitives ($ZodString, $ZodNumber, $ZodBoolean, $ZodBigInt, $ZodDate, $ZodSymbol), structural types ($ZodObject, $ZodArray, $ZodRecord, $ZodTuple, $ZodMap, $ZodSet), and logical types ($ZodUnion, $ZodDiscriminatedUnion, $ZodIntersection, $ZodEnum, $ZodLiteral). Covers the Classic vs Mini API differences.

pages.title: String Schemas and Formats
pages.purpose: Deep dive into $ZodString and its format sub-types: email, URL, UUID (v4/v6/v7), CUID, ULID, nanoid, IP addresses, base64, JWT, emoji, phone numbers (e164), and more. Covers the regexes module and string-specific checks (min/max length, trim, toLowerCase, etc.).

pages.title: Object Schemas and Modifiers
pages.purpose: Covers ZodObject in depth: strip/strict/passthrough modes, catchall, .extend(), .merge(), .pick(), .omit(), .partial(), .required(), and the JIT optimization ($ZodObjectJIT). Explains refinement safety rules for structural modifications.

pages.title: Checks and Refinements
pages.purpose: Explains the $ZodCheck system: built-in checks (LessThan, GreaterThan, MinLength, MaxLength, MultipleOf, Regex, etc.), the abort flag for short-circuiting, the when conditional, and user-defined refinements via .refine() and .superRefine(). Covers continuability and the check attachment lifecycle.

pages.title: Parsing Pipeline
pages.purpose: How parse(), safeParse(), parseAsync(), and safeParseAsync() work internally. Covers ParsePayload, ParseContextInternal, the run() vs parse() distinction, direction (forward/backward), the $ZodAsyncError, and how issues are finalized via finalizeIssue().

pages.title: Transforms, Pipes, and Codecs
pages.purpose: Explains unidirectional transforms (.transform()), pipe chaining ($ZodPipe), and bidirectional codecs ($ZodCodec with decode/encode). Covers the direction-aware architecture, when transforms are prohibited (backward direction), and the z.decode()/z.encode() API.

pages.title: Wrappers and Modifiers
pages.purpose: Covers schema wrappers: optional, nullable, nullish, default, prefault, catch, readonly, brand, promise, and lazy. Explains the difference between default (output-side) and prefault (input-side), and how branded types simulate nominal typing.

pages.title: Coercion and ISO Schemas
pages.purpose: Documents the z.coerce namespace (string, number, boolean, bigint, date) and the ISO sub-module (datetime, date, time, duration). Explains how coercion uses JavaScript constructors and the difference from standard schemas.

pages.title: Error Handling
pages.purpose: Overview of Zod's error system: ZodError, ZodIssue types, error formatting utilities, and internationalization. Links to child pages for error types, formatting, and customization.

pages.title: ZodError and Issue Types
pages.purpose: Reference for the $ZodError class and all $ZodIssue subtypes: invalid_type, too_big, too_small, invalid_format, not_multiple_of, unrecognized_keys, invalid_union, invalid_key, invalid_element, invalid_value, and custom. Covers the path array and how issues are collected during parsing.

pages.title: Error Formatting Utilities
pages.purpose: Documents z.treeifyError(), z.prettifyError(), z.formatError(), and z.flattenError(). Explains the $ZodFormattedError and $ZodFlattenedError types and when to use each utility.

pages.title: Error Customization and Localization
pages.purpose: Covers the $ZodErrorMap interface, the error precedence chain (schema-level > per-parse > global > locale), z.config() for global configuration, and the locales subsystem with 50+ language packs. Explains pluralization, grammatical gender handling, and tree-shaking locale imports.

pages.title: JSON Schema Integration
pages.purpose: Overview of Zod's bidirectional JSON Schema support: converting Zod schemas to JSON Schema and importing JSON Schema as Zod schemas. Links to child pages for each direction.

pages.title: Zod to JSON Schema (toJSONSchema)
pages.purpose: Documents the toJSONSchema() function: supported targets (draft-04, draft-07, draft-2020-12, openapi-3.0), io mode (input vs output), cycle handling, reused schema strategies ($defs), unrepresentable types, the override callback, and the Standard Schema integration (~standard property).

pages.title: JSON Schema to Zod (fromJSONSchema)
pages.purpose: Documents the fromJSONSchema() function: supported JSON Schema keywords, version detection, $ref/$defs resolution, and limitations. Covers the ConversionContext and how unrecognized keywords are handled.

pages.title: Metadata and Registries
pages.purpose: Explains the $ZodRegistry system for attaching strongly-typed metadata to schemas, the globalRegistry, .meta() and .describe() methods, the JSONSchemaMeta interface, and how registries integrate with toJSONSchema() for id/title/description propagation.

pages.title: Registry API
pages.purpose: Detailed API reference for $ZodRegistry: add(), get(), has(), remove(), clear(), id-based lookup via _idmap, metadata inheritance from parent schemas, and the $replace type utility for input/output-aware metadata.

pages.title: Standard Schema Compatibility
pages.purpose: Documents Zod's implementation of the Standard Schema V1 specification via the ~standard property. Covers StandardSchemaV1 interface, validate(), input/output type inference, and how this enables interoperability with other validation libraries.

pages.title: Package Variants
pages.purpose: Overview of the three Zod package variants (zod, zod/mini, zod/v4/core) and their trade-offs. Links to child pages for each variant.

pages.title: Zod (Classic)
pages.purpose: Documents the full-featured Zod package: the ZodType base class with fluent method chaining, all convenience methods (.min(), .max(), .email(), etc.), the coerce namespace, iso sub-module, and the compat layer for Zod v3 compatibility.

pages.title: Zod Mini
pages.purpose: Documents the tree-shakable Zod Mini variant: functional API (z.parse(schema, data) instead of schema.parse(data)), how methods become standalone functions, bundle size comparison, and when to choose Mini over Classic.

pages.title: Zod Core (zod/v4/core)
pages.purpose: Documents the zod/v4/core sub-package intended for library authors: $ZodType base class, $ZodCheck base class, $ZodError, the $constructor pattern, and how to build custom schema implementations on top of core.

pages.title: Ecosystem and Integrations
pages.purpose: Overview of Zod's ecosystem: third-party integrations, form libraries, API frameworks, schema converters, and official integration tests. Links to child pages for the ecosystem catalog and library author guidance.

pages.title: Ecosystem Catalog
pages.purpose: Categorized directory of third-party libraries: API frameworks (tRPC, oRPC), form integrations (react-hook-form, TanStack Form), Zod-to-X converters (OpenAPI, JSON Schema), X-to-Zod generators (Prisma, Drizzle), mocking libraries, and utilities. Covers the ZodResource data model and GitHub stars loader.

pages.title: Library Author Guide
pages.purpose: Best practices for library authors integrating with Zod: peer dependency configuration, using zod/v4/core for version-agnostic code, Standard Schema as an alternative, and the integration test fixtures (AI SDK, Drizzle ORM, internal types).

pages.title: Infrastructure and Tooling
pages.purpose: Overview of the monorepo infrastructure: build pipeline, CI/CD, testing, benchmarking, and type-checking tooling. Links to child pages for each area.
